data.user_message:
*settles into chair and sighs*

Right, so — I'm the Director of Employment Services at the Palestinian Ministry of Labor. What we're trying to do, in the simplest terms, is connect young Palestinians with jobs. That's the mission. But honestly, the thing that's keeping me up at night right now is that we have absolutely no idea what happens to these young people after they walk into one of our offices. They register with us, we help them put together a basic CV, and then... nothing. We never know if they found work, if their skills were matched to anything real, or if we actually helped them at all. We're supposed to be a public employment service, but we have nothing to show for it.
data.assistant_message: I understand the challenge you're facing. What are the three most important things a user must be able to do with the system you're envisioning?

critical_gaps.label: System Scope & Boundaries
critical_gaps.description: What is inside and outside the system boundary?

critical_gaps.label: Stakeholders & User Classes
critical_gaps.description: Who are the users and stakeholders of the system?

critical_gaps.label: Functional Requirements
critical_gaps.description: What must the system do? Core features and behaviours.

critical_gaps.label: Performance Requirements
critical_gaps.description: How fast must the system respond? What load must it handle?

critical_gaps.label: Usability & Accessibility
critical_gaps.description: How easy must the system be to use? Any accessibility requirements?

critical_gaps.label: Security & Privacy Requirements
critical_gaps.description: How must the system protect data and prevent unauthorised access?

critical_gaps.label: Reliability & Availability
critical_gaps.description: How reliable must the system be? What happens when it fails?

critical_gaps.label: Compatibility & Portability
critical_gaps.description: What platforms must the system run on? What must it integrate with?

critical_gaps.label: Maintainability & Extensibility
critical_gaps.description: How will the system be maintained, updated, and extended over time?

critical_gaps.label: User Registration Management
critical_gaps.description: How should someone sign up to use your system, like if a new job seeker wants to create an account?

data.user_message:
Honestly, I don't really think in those terms — I'm not a software person. I just know what's broken and what I wish were different.

I mean... if I had to say what matters most? First, I want a young person who walks into any of our offices — or maybe even goes online from home — to be able to put their information in once, properly, and have that actually mean something. Not just sit in a drawer somewhere.

And then I want that person to somehow end up in front of the right employer — without my staff having to make ten phone calls or guess based on who they happen to know.

But honestly, the third thing is for me and my team — I need to be able to answer questions like "how many young people found work this month, and in which sector?" Right now I can't answer that, and it's embarrassing when our international partners ask.
data.assistant_message: I see, you're looking for a more effective way to track and connect job seekers with employers. How should someone sign up to use your system, like if a new job seeker wants to create an account?
